message:
feat(wal): add segment file I/O for parallel WAL (D1.6)

Implements segment file I/O infrastructure for writing per-epoch WAL
batches to disk. Each epoch's flushed records are serialized to a
separate segment file, enabling parallel WAL recovery.

Key additions:
- FsyncPolicy enum (Full/Normal/Off) for durability control
- SegmentHeader struct with magic/version/epoch/checksum
- segment_path() generates "{db}-wal-seg-{epoch:016x}" paths
- write_segment() writes header + length-prefixed records with CRC32C
- read_segment() parses header and deserializes records
- list_segments() finds and sorts all segment files by epoch
- delete_segment() removes a segment file

The epoch ticker loop now writes segment files when flushing batches.
Segment format is designed for sequential access during recovery.

Tests added:
- Segment header roundtrip, invalid magic, checksum mismatch
- Segment path generation
- Segment write and read with record verification
- List segments with sorting

TODO for D1.7: Update recovery to replay multiple segments.

Epic: bd-3wop3 (Eliminate 8-16 Thread Contention)
Bead: bd-3wop3.1 (Per-thread WAL buffers + epoch group commit)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
